vocabulary.meaning: a long journey, especially by ship
vocabulary.example: The Titanic sank on its maiden voyage.

vocabulary.meaning: an act of traveling from one place to another, especially over a long distance or period of time
vocabulary.example: It was a long and difficult journey through the mountains.

vocabulary.meaning: a journey in which you go to a place, usually for a short time, and come back again
vocabulary.example: We took a weekend trip to the beach.

vocabulary.meaning: the activity of making journeys or traveling in general
vocabulary.example: Travel is a great way to learn about other cultures.

vocabulary.meaning: a short journey usually made for pleasure, often by a group of people
vocabulary.example: The school organized a day excursion to the museum.

vocabulary.meaning: the whole area that you can see from a particular place
vocabulary.example: The view from the top of the mountain is breathtaking.

vocabulary.meaning: something that you see, especially something interesting or beautiful
vocabulary.example: We visited all the famous sights of Paris.

vocabulary.meaning: the earth, together with all of its countries and peoples
vocabulary.example: He wants to travel around the world.

vocabulary.meaning: the planet on which we live; the land or soil
vocabulary.example: The earth is the third planet from the sun.

vocabulary.meaning: a particular part of a town, country, or region
vocabulary.example: This area is known for its beautiful lakes.

vocabulary.meaning: an area of land that is controlled by a particular country or leader
vocabulary.example: The pack of wolves defended their territory.

vocabulary.meaning: one of the four main periods of the year (spring, summer, autumn, winter)
vocabulary.example: My favorite season is autumn.

vocabulary.meaning: a length of time, or a division of time in a school day
vocabulary.example: We have history class in the first period.

vocabulary.meaning: the money that you pay to travel on a bus, train, taxi, plane, etc.
vocabulary.example: The train fare to London has gone up again.

vocabulary.meaning: a printed piece of paper showing that you have paid for a journey, entrance, or event
vocabulary.example: Did you buy the concert tickets yet?

vocabulary.meaning: an amount of money paid for a particular service or transaction
vocabulary.example: The lawyer charged a high fee for her advice.

vocabulary.meaning: to fail to catch a train, bus, plane, etc.
vocabulary.example: Hurry up, or we will miss the train!

vocabulary.meaning: to fail to keep or find something; to no longer have possession of
vocabulary.example: Be careful not to lose your passport.

vocabulary.meaning: to carry or move something or someone from one place to another
vocabulary.example: Can you take these bags to the car?

vocabulary.meaning: to take or carry someone or something to a place with you
vocabulary.example: Don't forget to bring an umbrella.

vocabulary.meaning: to move or travel from one place to another, leaving the starting position
vocabulary.example: We should go to bed early tonight.

vocabulary.meaning: to arrange to have a seat, room, or ticket at a particular time in the future
vocabulary.example: You need to book your flight well in advance.

vocabulary.meaning: to continue to have, own, or store something
vocabulary.example: She keeps all her old letters in a box.

vocabulary.meaning: to reach a place, especially at the end of a journey
vocabulary.example: The plane arrived at the airport on time.

vocabulary.meaning: to arrive at a destination (usually followed by a direct object)
vocabulary.example: We reached the city after a three-hour drive.

vocabulary.meaning: to have your home in a particular location permanently or for a long time
vocabulary.example: They live in a large house in the country.

vocabulary.meaning: to live in a place for a short time as a guest or visitor
vocabulary.example: We stayed at a cozy hotel during our holiday.

vocabulary.meaning: the official line that separates two countries or regions
vocabulary.example: We crossed the border into Canada.

vocabulary.meaning: the outer point or boundary of something, furthest from the center
vocabulary.example: Don't stand too close to the edge of the cliff.

vocabulary.meaning: a long, thin mark on a surface
vocabulary.example: Draw a straight line on the paper.

vocabulary.meaning: the measurement of something from end to end
vocabulary.example: What is the length of the swimming pool?

vocabulary.meaning: the amount of space between two places or points
vocabulary.example: The distance between the two cities is 100 miles.

vocabulary.meaning: to show someone the way by going with them
vocabulary.example: The local guide led the tourists through the jungle.

vocabulary.meaning: to direct or show the way, especially by going in front
vocabulary.example: You lead the way, and we will follow.

vocabulary.meaning: associated with the place of one's birth, or born and raised in a specific place
vocabulary.example: He is a native of New York.

vocabulary.meaning: the place where you live, or where you used to live when you were young
vocabulary.example: I am going home for Christmas.

vocabulary.meaning: very impressive, beautiful, or dramatic to look at
vocabulary.example: The fireworks display was spectacular.

vocabulary.meaning: a building with large sails that turn in the wind to generate power or grind grain
vocabulary.example: We saw traditional windmills in the Netherlands.

vocabulary.meaning: the outer edge of a round object, such as a wheel
vocabulary.example: He dented the rim of his bicycle wheel.

vocabulary.meaning: to move in the direction of a place
vocabulary.example: The boat made for the nearest harbor.

vocabulary.meaning: the first light of day in the morning
vocabulary.example: We woke up at dawn to start our journey.

vocabulary.meaning: a stage in a process of change or development
vocabulary.example: The project is now in its final phase.

vocabulary.meaning: a period or step in a process, or a platform for performers
vocabulary.example: This is a critical stage in the negotiations.

vocabulary.meaning: a foot-operated lever used to control or power a vehicle; to ride a bicycle
vocabulary.example: Push the pedal to accelerate the car.

vocabulary.meaning: to go to another place, get something, and bring it back
vocabulary.example: Can you fetch some water from the well?

vocabulary.meaning: to continue doing or using something without changing it
vocabulary.example: You should stick to your plan.

vocabulary.meaning: to connect A and B in your mind
vocabulary.example: Most people associate Switzerland with chocolate.

vocabulary.meaning: tourist spots that are no longer popular or well-maintained
vocabulary.example: The old amusement park had become a faded attraction.

vocabulary.meaning: to experience physical or mental pain or illness
vocabulary.example: She suffers from regular headaches.

vocabulary.meaning: the state of not being cared for properly; to fail to look after
vocabulary.example: The building fell into a state of neglect.

vocabulary.meaning: to intentionally pay no attention to someone or something
vocabulary.example: He ignored my warning.

vocabulary.meaning: exactly, accurately, and in detail
vocabulary.example: The model was made precisely to scale.

vocabulary.meaning: a very large and heavy building
vocabulary.example: The palace is a massive building.

vocabulary.meaning: exciting, unusual, and interesting because of being foreign
vocabulary.example: She loves exotic travel destinations.

vocabulary.word: none of us / neither of us
vocabulary.meaning: not any of a group of people / not one of two people
vocabulary.example: None of us knew the answer.

vocabulary.meaning: the imaginary line around the middle of the earth
vocabulary.example: Ecuador lies on the equator.

vocabulary.meaning: to write something on paper, or to let a passenger get out of a vehicle
vocabulary.example: The bus set the passengers down outside the station.

vocabulary.meaning: to put something in the wrong place and lose it temporarily
vocabulary.example: I misplaced my keys again.

vocabulary.meaning: modern, recent, or containing the latest information
vocabulary.example: Please keep this database up to date.

vocabulary.meaning: a trip made by car
vocabulary.example: It is a long car journey to the north.

vocabulary.meaning: a high-speed train that uses magnetic levitation
vocabulary.example: The maglev train is extremely fast.

vocabulary.meaning: an important event in the history or development of something
vocabulary.example: Reaching this agreement is a significant milestone.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: try to reach the same level, speed, or score as someone ahead of you
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You'll have to run fast to catch up with him.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: maintain the same speed or level as someone or something else
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She walked so fast that I could hardly keep up with her.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: register at a hotel or airport
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Please check in at the reception desk upon arrival.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: leave a hotel after paying the bill
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We need to check out of the hotel before noon.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: let someone get out of a vehicle, or fall asleep
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The bus driver dropped me off near my house.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: fall asleep, especially during the day or when you did not intend to
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I dozed off during the boring lecture.
EX: When did you get back from your holiday?
EX: I don't want to go back to that restaurant.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: go on holiday or leave a place
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We are going away for the weekend.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: head in the direction of a place
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: As soon as it started raining, we made for the nearest shelter.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: give someone a lift in a vehicle
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Can you pick me up from the station at 6 o'clock?

phrasalVerbs.meaning: stop a vehicle by the side of the road in a designated space
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The truck pulled in to let the cars pass.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: stop a vehicle by the side of the road
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The police officer ordered the driver to pull over.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: hit someone or something with a vehicle
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Be careful not to run over that dog in the road.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: go to a station or airport to say goodbye to someone
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: My parents came to the airport to see me off.
EX: They set out at dawn to climb the mountain.
EX: The plane took off on time despite the fog.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: change direction to go back the opposite way
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We realized we were lost and had to turn round.

collocations.meaning: be involved in a collision or crash
be in front of or more advanced than
a turn or shift in path

collocations.collocation: off the top of your head

collocations.meaning: go in the direction of a place
public holiday when banks are closed
in the corner on the left
using the left hand more naturally than the right

collocations.collocation: go on (/take) a tour of (a)round somewhere
collocations.meaning: take a guided visit of a place

wordPatterns.meaning: feeling fear or anxiety about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is afraid of flying.

wordPatterns.meaning: too scared or hesitant to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: I was afraid to ask him.

wordPatterns.meaning: seem to have a particular quality
wordPatterns.example: EX: He appears to be very tired.

wordPatterns.meaning: make plans about something with someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: I arranged a meeting with my manager.

wordPatterns.meaning: organize or plan for someone to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: They arranged for a taxi to pick us up.

wordPatterns.meaning: reach a country, city, or large location
wordPatterns.example: EX: We arrived in London on Tuesday.

wordPatterns.meaning: reach a specific building, station, or small location
wordPatterns.example: EX: The train arrived at the station on time.

wordPatterns.meaning: keep performing an action without stopping
wordPatterns.example: EX: He continued reading his book.

wordPatterns.meaning: keep performing an action or state
wordPatterns.example: EX: Prices continue to rise.

wordPatterns.meaning: proceed with an ongoing activity or project
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please continue with your work.

wordPatterns.meaning: be different or unlike something else
wordPatterns.example: EX: French differs from English in many ways.

wordPatterns.meaning: think of or imagine something while asleep or as a future goal
wordPatterns.example:
EX: I dreamed about you last night.
EX: She dreams of becoming a singer.

wordPatterns.meaning: ask someone politely to do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: They invited us to stay for dinner.

wordPatterns.meaning: very eager or enthusiastic to do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is keen to learn.

wordPatterns.meaning: very interested in or fond of an activity
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is keen on playing tennis.

wordPatterns.meaning: reside in a city/country or at a specific address
wordPatterns.example: EX: They live in a small village.

wordPatterns.meaning: depend on something for survival, or have it as your main purpose
wordPatterns.example:
EX: Some animals live on grass.
EX: He lives for his music.

wordPatterns.meaning: feel sad or sorry about something you did or did not do in the past
wordPatterns.example: EX: I regret not going to university.

wordPatterns.meaning: used to politely break bad news to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: We regret to inform you that your flight is cancelled.

wordPatterns.meaning: consider something or have it in mind
wordPatterns.example:
EX: What do you think of this idea?
EX: I am thinking about moving abroad.

wordPatterns.meaning: create a text concerning a specific subject
wordPatterns.example: EX: She wrote a book about her travels.

wordPatterns.meaning: send a letter or message to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: I wrote a letter to my friend.

wordPatterns.meaning: write and send a message directly to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: I'll write you a note.

wordPatterns.meaning: record information on paper
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please write down his address.

wordFormation.meaning: arrange: organize or make plans / arrangement: a plan or preparation / rearrange: organize differently
wordFormation.example:
EX: We arranged a meeting.
EX: They made travel arrangements.
EX: She rearranged the furniture.

wordFormation.meaning: arrive: reach a place / arrival: the act of reaching a place
wordFormation.example:
EX: The train arrived on time.
EX: We awaited the arrival of the guests.

wordFormation.meaning: broad: wide or general / breadth: width or distance from side to side / broaden: make wider or more general
wordFormation.example:
EX: He has broad shoulders.
EX: We measured the breadth of the field.
EX: Travel broadens the mind.

wordFormation.meaning: culture: ideas, customs, and social behavior of a society / cultured: educated, refined, and knowledgeable / cultural: relating to culture
wordFormation.example:
EX: She is interested in art and culture.
EX: He is a very cultured man who loves classical music.
EX: The town has a rich cultural heritage.

wordFormation.meaning: distant: far away in space or time / distance: amount of space between two points
wordFormation.example:
EX: The stars are very distant.
EX: What is the distance between Hanoi and Saigon?

wordFormation.meaning: differ: be unlike or disagree / difference: a way in which things are not the same / different: not the same / indifferent: having no interest or sympathy
wordFormation.example:
EX: They differ on many issues.
EX: There is a big difference between them.
EX: They have different opinions.
EX: She was indifferent to his problems.

wordFormation.meaning: direct: straight or without stopping / indirect: not straight or roundabout / direction: course along which someone moves / director: manager or administrator
wordFormation.example:
EX: This is a direct flight to Paris.
EX: They took an indirect route.
EX: Which direction are we going?
EX: He is the director of the film.

wordFormation.meaning: enter: go or come into a place / entrance: door, gate, or passage to enter
wordFormation.example:
EX: Please enter through the back door.
EX: The entrance to the museum is free.

wordFormation.meaning: inhabit: live or reside in / inhabitant: person or animal that lives in a place
wordFormation.example:
EX: Polar bears inhabit the Arctic.
EX: The island has only a few inhabitants.

wordFormation.meaning: photograph: a picture taken by a camera / photographer: person who takes pictures / photography: art or practice of taking pictures / photographic: relating to photography
wordFormation.example:
EX: I took a photograph of the castle.
EX: She works as a professional photographer.
EX: He is studying photography.
EX: They bought new photographic equipment.

wordFormation.meaning: recognise / recognize: identify from having seen before / recognition: act of identifying or appreciating / recognisable / recognizable: easy to identify
wordFormation.example:
EX: I recognized her immediately.
EX: He received an award in recognition of his service.
EX: The building was easily recognizable.

wordFormation.meaning: time: continuous progress of existence / timetable: schedule of times when events occur
wordFormation.example:
EX: Time passes very quickly.
EX: Have you checked the train timetable?

wordFormation.meaning: tour: a journey for pleasure or business / tourism: business of organizing travel for tourists / tourist: person who travels for pleasure
wordFormation.example:
EX: We went on a tour of the city.
EX: Tourism is vital for the local economy.
EX: The hotel was packed with tourists.

wordFormation.meaning: world: the earth and all life on it / worldwide: extending throughout the entire world
wordFormation.example:
EX: They want to travel the world.
EX: The news spread worldwide.
